2605.09542.title: LLM-Guided Monte Carlo Tree Search over Knowledge Graphs: Composing Mechanistic Explanations for Drug-Disease Pairs
2605.09542.paper_summary: Extracting multi-step explanations from knowledge graphs poses a combinatorial challenge requiring both heuristic guidance (as candidates proliferate with depth) and credit assignment (as path quality emerges over extended sequences). Frontier LLMs, strong on knowledge/reasoning benchmarks, offer a compelling source of such heuristics, yet their knowledge comes sans guarantees and compositional performance degrades as chains lengthen. We thus present TESSERA, a 3-part neuro-symbolic framework that uses LLMs in a circumscribed role: for local discriminative judgement rather than autonomous multi-step generation; the knowledge graph then defines the hypothesis space enforcing hard structural constraints, and MCTS coordinates the long-horizon search with principled credit assignment via backpropagation. LLMs perform dual roles as a prior policy biasing exploration and a comparative state evaluator supplying reward signals. Evaluation on drug mechanism elucidation across two complementary knowledge graphs demonstrates fidelity to curated biology while surfacing coherent alternative mechanisms, with ablations confirming discriminative contribution from both LLM components. Beyond its current application, our framework offers a general paradigm for compositional reasoning over structured knowledge.
2605.09542.paper_summary_zh: 從知識圖譜中提取多步解釋提出了一個組合挑戰，這需要啟發式指導（因為隨著深度增加，候選者會大量增加）和信用分配（因為隨著序列延長，路徑質量會顯現）。前沿的大型語言模型在知識/推理基準上表現強勁，提供了這些啟發式的引人注目的來源，但它們的知識並不保證，且隨著鏈條的延長，組合性能會下降。因此，我們提出了TESSERA，一個三部分的神經符號框架，將大型語言模型用於有限的角色：用於局部的判別判斷，而非自主的多步生成；知識圖譜則定義了假設空間，強制施加嚴格的結構約束，而蒙特卡羅樹搜索協調著長期的搜索，通過反向傳播進行原則性的信用分配。大型語言模型在這裡扮演雙重角色，一方面作為先驗策略來偏向探索，另一方面作為比較狀態評估者提供獎勵信號。在兩個互補的知識圖譜上對藥物機制的評估顯示了對策劃生物學的忠實，同時提出了連貫的替代機制，消融實驗確認了兩個大型語言模型組件的判別貢獻。超越當前的應用，我們的框架為結構化知識上的組合推理提供了一個通用範式。
2605.09542.author: Rishabh Jakhar et.al.
2605.09542.authors: Rishabh Jakhar, Michel Dumontier, Remzi Celebi
